title: PDRM Intelligence: Parcel Delivery Scam via SMS and Phone
content: PDRM CCID reports a widespread parcel delivery scam where victims receive SMS or calls claiming a parcel is held at customs or a delivery depot. The message instructs victims to click a link or call a number to arrange redelivery. The link leads to a phishing site mimicking Pos Malaysia, DHL, or J&T Express, asking for personal details and payment of a 'customs clearance fee' or 'redelivery charge'. In phone call variants, the scammer transfers the victim to a fake 'customs officer' who threatens legal action for importing prohibited items. The victim is then advised to transfer money to a 'security account'. Common SMS texts include: 'Pos Malaysia: Your parcel MY123456 is held at customs. Pay RM 15.90 to release: [link]', 'J&T Express: We attempted delivery but failed. Reschedule at [link]'. In 2025, parcel scams affected over 5,600 victims with losses of RM 32 million. Pos Malaysia confirms they never request payment via SMS links. Always verify directly through the courier's official app.
